commit.message:
Use EndpointSlices API for webhook readiness check (#625)

Migrates from deprecated Endpoints to EndpointSlices API. Uses kubectl get instead of kubectl wait to correctly check if any endpoint is ready, avoiding kubectl wait's JSONPath limitation where wildcard expressions fail with multiple webhook replicas.

verification.payload:
tree 934587dad3ef632651cb438f822b6370af8a7ada
parent 88488715ac0d6dbb5ee54039ed4adb3a280c4832
author Vincent Link <linkvt@users.noreply.github.com> 1768824473 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1768824473 +0000

Use EndpointSlices API for webhook readiness check (#625)

Migrates from deprecated Endpoints to EndpointSlices API. Uses kubectl get instead of kubectl wait to correctly check if any endpoint is ready, avoiding kubectl wait's JSONPath limitation where wildcard expressions fail with multiple webhook replicas.

commit.message:
fix: update minimum k8 version to fix timeouts (#602)

The default minimum k8 version for knative changed and is listed here:
https://github.com/knative/pkg/blob/main/version/version.go#L36

Bump up k8, kind, and minikube versions and a comment with the link
above. Add versions to the main `README.md` and fix a broken link.

Issues #601 #600.

Signed-off-by: Stanislav Jakuschevskij <stas@two-giants.com>

issues.title: Local registry doc nit
issues.body:
This line in README should be moved to the new registry section:

> Note: we automatically configure tag resolution for the local registry when this flag is passed

 _Originally posted by @joebowbeer in [61316f9](https://github.com/knative-extensions/kn-plugin-quickstart/commit/61316f9f90d7bb09f3b0ca65888c3636171794bb#r173580281)_

issues.title: Should refer the socket selected in current docker context instead of  /var/run/docker.sock
issues.body:
When I tried `kn quickstart kind` command, it gave a following error
```
failed to create kind cluster: failed to get Docker info: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix:///var/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?
Error: exit status 1
```

This seems because I use different socket than default (`/var/run/docker.sock`) and there is no socket working on `/var/run/docker.sock`
```
docker context ls
NAME            DESCRIPTION                               DOCKER ENDPOINT                                           ERROR
default         Current DOCKER_HOST based configuration   unix:///var/run/docker.sock                               
lima-docker *                                             unix:///Users/teppei.sudo/.lima/docker/sock/docker.sock  
```

I am not sure how `kn quickstart kind` command is working but `kind` command itself is working fine with this non-default context and its endpoint/socket.

after I created symbolic link on `/var/run/docker.sock`, `kn quickstart kind` command was successful.
```
ln -sf /Users/teppei.sudo/.lima/docker/sock/docker.sock /var/run/docker.sock
```

Conclusion: 
I expect `kn quickstart kind` command to use the socket that is selected in **current docker context** instead of `/var/run/docker.sock`

issues.title: Serving webhook connection failure despite readiness check passing

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

issues.body:
Hi,

I was working with latest version by using the main branch to get around the limitations fixed in #602 (a release would be greatly appreciated as quickstart is broken for every user currently 😅 ) and likely found a bug in the current webhook readiness check implementation from #607 .

It seems that the webhook check only checks for ready Pods to exist and not if Endpoints of the webhook Service are available.
It's only a short time between the pod being ready and the service being available by having endpoints but it was enough to fail for me occasionally with the following logs:


```console
❯ ./kn-quickstart kind --registry -k 1.34.0
Running Knative Quickstart using Kind
✅ Checking dependencies...
    Kind version is: 0.30.0
💽 Installing local registry...
...
enabling experimental podman provider
Creating cluster "knative" ...
 ✓ Ensuring node image (kindest/node:v1.34.0) 🖼
 ✓ Preparing nodes 📦
 ✓ Writing configuration 📜
 ✓ Starting control-plane 🕹️
 ✓ Installing CNI 🔌
 ✓ Installing StorageClass 💾
 ✓ Waiting ≤ 2m0s for control-plane = Ready ⏳
 • Ready after 16s 💚
Set kubectl context to "kind-knative"
You can now use your cluster with:

kubectl cluster-info --context kind-knative

Have a question, bug, or feature request? Let us know! https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/#community 🙂

🔗 Patching node: knative-control-plane
🍿 Installing Knative Serving v1.19.6 ...
    CRDs installed...
    Core installed...
    Waiting for webhook to be ready...
    Webhook is ready...
Error from server (InternalError): Internal error occurred: failed calling webhook "config.webhook.serving.knative.dev": failed to call webhook: Post "https://webhook.knative-serving.svc:443/config-validation?timeout=10s": dial tcp 10.96.107.170:443: connect: connection refused

Error: failed to install serving to kind cluster knative: tag resolving configuration: exit status 1
```

I'll create a PR to check for endpoints but as it is a bit hard to reproduce the problem might not be 100% fixed.

Thanks, Vincent

/kind bug

This is a similar bug to #603. The difference here is that it does not get to the Kourier installation but fails after the installation of Serving finished. issues.title: Bug: `kn quickstart kind -k 1.34.0` fails to deploy Kourier in kind cluster with `kubectl v1.34`.

labels.name: kind/bug

issues.body:
## Bug Summary

Using `kn quickstart kind -k 1.34.0` fails to finish the deployment when installing Kourier. It breaks when it should be waiting for the pods to get ready right here in [install.go#L36](https://github.com/knative-extensions/kn-plugin-quickstart/blob/993888519b82ab6281306960449c6566e41116c6/pkg/install/install.go#L36). I was not able to figure out why. The pod `3scale-kourier-gateway-54fb555bd7-qkzn7` actually comes into a running state eventually.

When I start kind and deploy Serving and Eventing with the [shell script](https://gist.github.com/dsimansk/db18edd4759f5a1e218cfebe162f2c48) from @dsimansk it runs through. I just removed the podman environment variable.

It looks like the issue is `kubectl v1.34`. Running the same command with `kubectl v1.33.5` runs without any issues.

## System Info
 
- OS: Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition)
- kubectl: client 1.34.0, server 1.34.0

- go: 1.25.0
- docker engine: 28.4.0

issues.title: `kn quickstart kind` not working with newer versions of k8s

labels.name: kind/bug
labels.description: Categorizes issue or PR as related to a bug.

labels.name: triage/accepted
labels.description: Issues which should be fixed (post-triage)

issues.body:
## Summary

Running `kn quickstart kind` runs into a timeout:

```shell
 2025/09/06 16:06:16 Failed to get k8s version kubernetes version "1.31.6" is not compatible, need at least "1.32.0-0" (this can be overridden with the env var "KUBERNETES_MIN_VERSION")
``` 

Related issue #480.

## Solution

A quick fix is to use the kubernetes version option: `kn quickstart kind -k 1.34.0`

A better fix would be a bump of used versions like in this PR #479 or a dynamic setting of versions depending on the knative components.

issues.title: Broken link in the documentation.

labels.name: triage/accepted
labels.description: Issues which should be fixed (post-triage)

issues.body:
## Summary

In the main `README.md` in _Building from Source_ the first link "set up your development environment" is broken.

## Solution

It looks like this should be the correct link: https://github.com/knative/client/blob/main/DEVELOPMENT.md#prerequisites.

issues.title: Installing Kourier fails with "error: no matching resources found"
issues.body:
`kn quickstart kind` fails with the following error:

```console
$ kn quickstart kind
...

    Finished installing Knative Serving
🕸️ Installing Kourier networking layer v1.18.0 ...
error: no matching resources found

Error: install kourier: kourier: exit status 1
```

It seems the error occurres here:

https://github.com/knative-extensions/kn-plugin-quickstart/blob/v0.45.0/pkg/install/install.go#L36-L38

This might be because the following command is temporary failing:

```console
$ kubectl wait pod --timeout=10m --for=condition=Ready -l '!job-name' -n kourier-system
```

However, the same command will succeed if I run it by hands immediately after the failure.

```
$ kn quickstart kind
...
Error: install kourier: kourier: exit status 1

$ kubectl wait pod --timeout=10m --for=condition=Ready -l '!job-name' -n kourier-system

...(a mitute later)...

pod/3scale-kourier-gateway-5bb4856d57-p7wwx condition met
```

This migmt be related to this issue?

- https://github.com/kubernetes/kubectl/issues/1675

It was fixed for `kubectl@v1.33.0`. 

I installed `kubectl@v1.33.2` using Homebrew (I use Mac), but it does not fix the Kourier installation problem.

### Environment

```console
$ kn version

Build Date:   2025-04-23 19:42:52
Git Revision: 96721e59
Supported APIs:
* Serving
  - serving.knative.dev/v1 (knative-serving v1.18.0)
* Eventing

  - eventing.knative.dev/v1 (knative-eventing v1.18.0)

$ kn plugin list
- kn-func : /opt/homebrew/bin/kn-func
- kn-quickstart : /opt/homebrew/bin/kn-quickstart

$ /opt/homebrew/bin/kn-quickstart version
Version:      v1.18.0
Build Date:   2025-04-24 09:08:55
Git Revision: 86a9619

$ kubectl version --client
Client Version: v1.33.2
Kustomize Version: v5.6.0

$ which kubectl
/opt/homebrew/bin/kubectl

$ kind version
kind v0.29.0 go1.24.3 darwin/arm64

## macOS
$ sw_vers
ProductName:		macOS
ProductVersion:		15.5
BuildVersion:		24F74
```

Docker Desktop for Mac v4.42.1

issues.body:
<!--

e.g. `kn quickstart minikube -- --ports=8080`

Should be simple to support by just passing everything after a `--` to `minikube start`

issues.title: Can't pull images from local registry

labels.name: lifecycle/stale
labels.description: Denotes an issue or PR has remained open with no activity and has become stale.

issues.body:
<!-- 
Are you using Knative? If you do, we would love to know!
https://github.com/knative/community/issues/new?template=ADOPTERS.yaml&title=%5BADOPTERS%5D%3A+%24%7BCOMPANY+NAME+HERE%7D
-->

We're missing configuration from the `/etc/containerd/config.toml` to allow kind to pull images from the local registry spun up by `--registry`.

I'll open a PR to add the configuration as part of the `containerdConfigPatches` in `kind.go`.

The required configuration is:

```
    [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.mirrors]
      [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.mirrors."kind-registry:5000"]
        endpoint = ["http://kind-registry:5000"]
    [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs]
      [plugins."io.containerd.grpc.v1.cri".registry.configs."kind-registry:5000".tls]
        insecure_skip_verify = true
```

This configuration is required, e.g., for Apache Camel-K to be used with `kn quickstart`. Without this configuration, no images can be pulled from the local registry.

issues.title: How to use the kind local registry?

labels.name: triage/accepted
labels.description: Issues which should be fixed (post-triage)

issues.body: Just a question: how does the kind local image registry works with knative? Perhaps could we have a very little section in the `kn quickstart kind` docs explaining how to go from "I have an image built in my local docker/podman daemon" to "kn service create"?

issues.body: As [discussed on Slack](https://knative.slack.com/archives/C93E33SN8/p1654795211011919?thread_ts=1654794336.212679&cid=C93E33SN8), our error messages could be made more descriptive of what the actual error is, esp. in regard to kind/minikube errors.

pullRequests.title: Allow custom flags to be passed to minikube
pullRequests.body:
<!-- Thanks for sending a pull request! -->

<!-- 
Are you using Knative? If you do, we would love to know!
https://github.com/knative/community/issues/new?template=ADOPTERS.yaml&title=%5BADOPTERS%5D%3A+%24%7BCOMPANY+NAME+HERE%7D
-->

# Changes

<!-- 
Describe your changes here- ideally you can get that description straight from
your descriptive commit message(s)! 

- :gift: Add new feature
-->

- Allow custom minikube flags to be passed to minikube quickstart command

<!--
In addition, categorize the changes you're making using the "/kind" Prow command, example:

/kind <kind>

Supported kinds are: api-change, bug, cleanup, deprecation, removal, documentation, enhancement, performance

-->
/kind enhancement

<!--
*Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`.
_If PR is about `failing-tests or flakes`, please post the related issues/tests in a comment and do not use `Fixes`_*
-->
Fixes #557 

<!-- Please include the 'why' behind your changes if no issue exists -->

**Release Note**

<!--
:page_facing_up: If this change has user-visible impact, write a release note in the block
below. Include the string "action required" if additional action is required of
users switching to the new release, for example in case of a breaking change.

Write as if you are speaking to users, not other Knative contributors. If this
change has no user-visible impact, no release-note is needed.
-->
```release-note
"kn quickstart minikube" now accepts custom minikube flags passed, for example:

kn quickstart minikube -- ports=5000

```

**Docs**

<!--
:book: If this change has user-visible impact, link to an issue or PR in
https://github.com/knative/docs.

See here for guidance on getting permanent links to files: https://help.github.com/en/articles/getting-permanent-links-to-files

Please use the following format for linking documentation:
- [knative/docs]: <issue or pr link>
- [Feature Track]: <link>
- [Usage]: <link>
- [Other doc]: <link>
-->
```docs

```

pullRequests.title: Use EndpointSlices API for webhook readiness check
pullRequests.body:
Migrates from deprecated Endpoints to EndpointSlices API.

Tested locally by creating another cluster, happy path was successful.

<!-- Thanks for sending a pull request! -->

<!-- 
Are you using Knative? If you do, we would love to know!
https://github.com/knative/community/issues/new?template=ADOPTERS.yaml&title=%5BADOPTERS%5D%3A+%24%7BCOMPANY+NAME+HERE%7D
-->

# Changes

- Use EndpointSlices API for webhook readiness check instead of deprecated Endpoints


/kind bug

<!--
*Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`.
_If PR is about `failing-tests or flakes`, please post the related issues/tests in a comment and do not use `Fixes`_*
-->
Relates to #610 
Relates to #611

pullRequests.title: fix: check serving webhook readiness through endpoint lookup
pullRequests.body:
# Changes

<!-- 
Describe your changes here- ideally you can get that description straight from
your descriptive commit message(s)! 

- :gift: Add new feature
- :bug: Fix bug
- :broom: Update or clean up current behavior
- :wastebasket: Remove feature or internal logic
-->

- Checks the serving webhook readiness by looking at the endpoints and not ready pods
  - The failure scenario is a bit hard to reproduce, appreciate some testing/feedback

I see this issue consistently, almost every run of `kn quickstart kind ...` fails for me without this check due to the webhook being recognized as ready when it isn't reachable.

/kind bug


Fixes #610 
Related to #607 

<!-- Please include the 'why' behind your changes if no issue exists -->

**Release Note**

<!--
:page_facing_up: If this change has user-visible impact, write a release note in the block
below. Include the string "action required" if additional action is required of
users switching to the new release, for example in case of a breaking change.

Write as if you are speaking to users, not other Knative contributors. If this
change has no user-visible impact, no release-note is needed.
-->

```release-note
Fix webhook connection errors occuring sometimes after the webhook readiness check has passed.
```

pullRequests.title: Fix: update minimum k8 version to fix timeouts, add versions to documentation and fix broken link.
pullRequests.body:
<!-- Thanks for sending a pull request! -->

<!-- 
Are you using Knative? If you do, we would love to know!
https://github.com/knative/community/issues/new?template=ADOPTERS.yaml&title=%5BADOPTERS%5D%3A+%24%7BCOMPANY+NAME+HERE%7D
-->

# Changes

<!-- 
Describe your changes here- ideally you can get that description straight from
your descriptive commit message(s)! 

- :gift: Add new feature
- :bug: Fix bug
- :broom: Update or clean up current behavior
- :wastebasket: Remove feature or internal logic
-->

The default minimum k8 version for knative changed and is listed here: https://github.com/knative/pkg/blob/main/version/version.go#L36.

- Update Kubernetes version to v1.32.0.
- Update minimum minikube version to v1.35.
- Add comment with link to default minimum kubernetes version needed by knative.
- Fix broken link in docs.
- Add versions of kubectl, kind and minikube to `README.md`.

<!--
In addition, categorize the changes you're making using the "/kind" Prow command, example:

/kind <kind>

Supported kinds are: api-change, bug, cleanup, deprecation, removal, documentation, enhancement, performance

-->
/kind bug
/kind documentation

<!--
*Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`.
_If PR is about `failing-tests or flakes`, please post the related issues/tests in a comment and do not use `Fixes`_*
-->
Fixes #601.
Fixes #600.

<!-- Please include the 'why' behind your changes if no issue exists -->

**Release Note**

<!--
:page_facing_up: If this change has user-visible impact, write a release note in the block
below. Include the string "action required" if additional action is required of
users switching to the new release, for example in case of a breaking change.

Write as if you are speaking to users, not other Knative contributors. If this
change has no user-visible impact, no release-note is needed.
-->
```release-note
Quickstart now uses Kubernetes version 1.32.0 as a default. 
The minimum recommended versions for Minikube is v1.35.
Documentation is updated to contain kubectl, kind and minikube versions.
```

pullRequests.title: Update outdated Kourier url
pullRequests.body:
<!-- Thanks for sending a pull request! -->

<!-- 
Are you using Knative? If you do, we would love to know!
https://github.com/knative/community/issues/new?template=ADOPTERS.yaml&title=%5BADOPTERS%5D%3A+%24%7BCOMPANY+NAME+HERE%7D
-->

# Changes


<!-- 
Describe your changes here- ideally you can get that description straight from
your descriptive commit message(s)! 

- :gift: Add new feature
- :bug: Fix bug
- :broom: Update or clean up current behavior
- :wastebasket: Remove feature or internal logic
-->

- Updates the Kourier Github url to reflect the renaming of knative-sandbox to knative-extensions.

<!--
In addition, categorize the changes you're making using the "/kind" Prow command, example:

/kind 

Supported kinds are: api-change, bug, cleanup, deprecation, removal, documentation, enhancement, performance

-->
/kind bug, deprecation

<!--
*Automatically closes linked issue when PR is merged.
Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`.
_If PR is about `failing-tests or flakes`, please post the related issues/tests in a comment and do not use `Fixes`_*
-->

The old url breaks environments using mirrors where automatic redirection is not in place. Mirrors are sometimes used in regulated industries which need to control code dependencies.

**Release Note**

<!--
:page_facing_up: If this change has user-visible impact, write a release note in the block
below. Include the string "action required" if additional action is required of
users switching to the new release, for example in case of a breaking change.

Write as if you are speaking to users, not other Knative contributors. If this
change has no user-visible impact, no release-note is needed.

-->
```release-note
Updated the Kourier GitHub URL to support users in air-gapped or mirrored environments.
```

<!--**Docs**-->

<!--
:book: If this change has user-visible impact, link to an issue or PR in

See here for guidance on getting permanent links to files: https://help.github.com/en/articles/getting-permanent-links-to-files

- [knative/docs]: <issue or pr link>

Default Kubernetes version is now v1.31.6

cc @knative-extensions/client-writers

pullRequests.title: EN implement robust minikube with more options and networking fixes
pullRequests.body:
# Changes
Added new support for Minikube!
- New flags for setup command
  - registryPort
  - extraMountHostPath
  - extraMountContainerPath
- Sets Knative domain 127.0.0.1.nip.io for host machine access
- Uses Docker driver by default
- Enables registry addon and registry support
- Auto runs Minikube tunnel in the background
- Sets clusterName as the default Minikube profile
- Ensures Kourier uses LoadBalancer 

Fixes #
- Fixes bug with ingress class - now correctly installs Kourier
  - removes: `ingress-class: istio.ingress.networking.knative.dev`
  - adds: `ingress-class: kourier.ingress.networking.knative.dev`

releases.name: v1.20.0
releases.body:
## Changes by Kind

### Documentation

- Quickstart now uses Kubernetes version 1.32.0 as a default. 
  The minimum recommended versions for Minikube is v1.35.
  Documentation is updated to contain kubectl, kind and minikube versions. (#602, @twoGiants)

### Uncategorized

- Fixes issue when creating a registry with Kubernetes v1.34+ (#607, @psschwei)

## Dependencies

### Added

issueComments.body:
Out of curiosity, do you just need this to reach a port or are there other configs you'd like to set on minikube too? 

Not opposed to this in principle, just trying to get a sense of your needs.

I happened to try adding a port for some UDP service, but there are plenty that are useful https://minikube.sigs.k8s.io/docs/commands/start/, e.g. `image-repository` to use a mirror, `gpus` to allow gpus, and so on, there are many different flags which is why I suggested a passthrough rather than trying to wrap every single flag minikube ever or will implement.

I can try a solution for this

/assign

prReviews.body:
looks pretty good. 

one small nit: using error in the logs can be a little redundant (and repetitive)... for example, if the kind cluster can't be created, we'll get something like:

`Error: error creating kind cluster: error checking kind version: failed to get kind version: exec: "kind": executable file not found in $PATH`

Could you swap out `error` for something else? (you've got `failed to get...` and `unable to...` other places, both of which are good).
